title: ClientProtocol.java
description: This Java interface, ClientProtocol, facilitates communication between JobClient and JobTracker in Hadoop MapReduce. It enables job submission, status monitoring, and resource management. Key methods include job submission, status retrieval, job killing, priority setting, and cluster status inquiry. It also manages delegation tokens and retrieves system directory paths. Inputs include JobID, Credentials, JobSubmitDir, TaskAttemptID, JobType, and Text. Outputs encompass JobStatus, ClusterMetrics, JobTrackerStatus, TaskReport[], String (filesystem name), JobStatus[], TaskCompletionEvent[], String[], TaskTrackerInfo[], String (system directory), String (staging area directory), String (job history directory), QueueInfo[], QueueInfo, QueueAclsInfo[], QueueInfo[], Token (for delegation tokens). Dependencies include IOException, VersionedProtocol, Text, Credentials, JobID, JobStatus, TaskAttemptID, TaskCompletionEvent, TaskReport, TaskType, DelegationTokenSelector, DelegationTokenIdentifier, JTConfig, LogParams, AccessControlList, Token. Collaborator classes are Cluster, Job, JobTracker, TaskTracker, DelegationTokenSelector, Token, AccessControlList, TokenInfo, and LogParams. The architectural role of this file is to establish a standardized communication protocol between JobClient and JobTracker, adhering to the Remote Procedure Call (RPC) pattern.
